items.q: Why do people move from rural to urban areas in LICs?
items.correct: A combination of push factors (rural poverty, lack of services, agricultural change) and pull factors (perceived jobs, education, healthcare) — though reality often disappoints.
items.misconception: Because cities in poor countries are always better than the countryside.
items.random_wrong: Because the government forces them to move to cities.
items.why: Migrants often end up in shanty towns worse than what they left. The decision is based on perception of opportunity, not guaranteed improvement — a key nuance students miss.

items.q: What is the difference between primary and secondary effects of an earthquake?
items.correct: Primary effects occur immediately as a direct result of shaking (building collapse, deaths); secondary effects follow afterwards (fires, tsunamis, disease, homelessness).
items.misconception: Primary effects are the worst effects and secondary effects are minor.
items.random_wrong: Primary effects happen first and secondary effects happen the next day.
items.why: Secondary effects can be far worse — the 2011 Tohoku tsunami killed more than the shaking itself. The distinction is about cause (direct vs cascading), not severity or strict timing.

items.q: Why has the Richter scale been largely replaced by the moment magnitude scale?
items.correct: Richter saturates above magnitude ~7 and was calibrated for local Californian quakes; moment magnitude (Mw) measures the total energy released and works for all sizes globally.
items.misconception: The Richter scale is still the standard scale used by all seismologists.
items.random_wrong: Moment magnitude measures damage while Richter measures shaking.
items.why: Media still says 'Richter' but seismologists now report Mw. Both are logarithmic — a magnitude 7 releases ~32x more energy than a 6 — but Mw is the modern standard for large quakes.
It's Pompeii. “Rome” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: Ash grounded flights. “Lava flooded London” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What year did Vesuvius destroy Pompeii (CE)?
items.why: The real figure is 79. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
